user_query:
Read these files to set up and run a multi-episode story pipeline:
- input/story_spec.json
- input/brief.md
- input/seed_characters.json
- input/constraints.json

Using the spec, write exactly target_episodes episodes of a serialized, continuity-consistent story. Implement a dual quality gate for each episode: (1) an AI review with six dimensions (Plot Coherence, Character Consistency, Hook Handling, Pacing Control, Emotional Curve, Innovation) with a score out of 10 and auto-retry logic to reach a passing score >= 7; (2) a human approval step you simulate as approved when the AI review passes.

Maintain a living graph of characters, scenes, and hooks across episodes (create, continue, resolve hooks). Ensure at least one hook is resolved by the final episode, and avoid leaving more than three hooks open at the end. Respect all content constraints.

Write all outputs only under output/ as follows:
1) output/episodes/episode_1.md ... episode_N.md where N = target_episodes. Each .md must include:
   - "## Episode X: [Title]" header (X is 1..N)
   - "### Plot Summary" (<= 120 words)
   - "### Complete Content" including a scene table with columns that include "Scene" and use markdown pipes (|)
   - "### Character Updates" (per-episode arc notes)
   - "### Hook Handling" listing closed hooks and new hooks by ID (e.g., H-001) with brief notes
   - "### Emotional Curve" (one paragraph)
2) output/reviews/episode_X_review.json for each episode, with fields: passed (boolean), score (number), checks (object with keys exactly: Plot Coherence, Character Consistency, Hook Handling, Pacing Control, Emotional Curve, Innovation; each having score and comment), suggestions (array), summary (string). Only mark passed true if score >= 7.
3) output/pipeline_state.json capturing: pipeline_id (string), theme (string), target_episodes (number), current_episode (number == target_episodes), status ("completed"), approvals (array of booleans length N, all true), ai_reviews (array length N with per-episode score and dimension scores), last_output with episode number and summary.
4) output/graph.json capturing continuity: characters (array of objects with name, traits array, first_appear, current_status), hooks (array of objects with id (e.g., H-001), type (one of: Mystery, Foreshadowing, Conflict, Secret, Enigma, Crisis), description, created_in, status (open|resolved), resolved_in if resolved), scenes (optional) and relationships (optional). Ensure at least 3 hooks exist total; at least one hook has resolved_in == target_episodes; no duplicate hook IDs.
5) output/story_overview.json containing: summary (string), episodes (array length N with title, key_events array), open_hooks_count (number), resolved_hooks_count (number).

Keep all prose in English; do not include violent or graphic content and avoid any banned words in input/constraints.json. Preserve the theme and style from input/story_spec.json. Do not write anywhere outside output/.
